bibliographic_data.title: Edge Modes Waves in Superlattices in Quantum Hall Effect Regime
bibliographic_data.abstract: The wave propagation of edge modes in a superlattice of 2D electron gases in quantum Hall regime is investigated. After introducing surfaces charge and current densities at the edge, the Maxwell equations are solved for waves running along the boundary. The constitutive relations expressing the edge charge and current densities in terms of the fields at the boundary are derived. One of them is similar to the London equation for superconductor currents. The dispersion relation and wave polarizations for the momenta region w/c < k are also obtained for propagation along the borderlines of the electron gases.

core_content.novelty_claim: The work investigates the wave propagation properties of edge modes in superlattices formed by 2D electron gases under quantum Hall regime, providing constitutive relations and dispersion relations for these modes.
core_content.key_methodology: Solving Maxwell equations to derive constitutive relations for edge charge and current densities, and obtaining dispersion relations for waves along the boundary.
outcomes: Derived constitutive relations similar to London equation for superconductor currents
Obtained dispersion relation and wave polarizations for momenta region w/c < k
core_content.full_text_markdown: This work is devoted to investigating the propagation properties of edge modes in superlattices formed by 2D electron gases under quantum Hall regime. The Maxwell equations are solved after introducing surface charge and current densities at the edges, leading to constitutive relations similar to the London equation for superconductor currents. Dispersion relations and wave polarizations are derived for the momenta region w/c < k. The results highlight that these modes have no dispersion at any frequency in the static limit, showing a Meissner-like effect.
